summary: Full backend + frontend regression for Joxera auth (JWT + Emergent Google OAuth). 8/8 pytest backend cases pass; all 5 required frontend flows verified via Playwright on the live preview URL.

issues: Non-blocking runtime error surfaced via window.onerror: 'Incorrect locale information provided' (Intl formatter called with an unsupported/empty locale). Does not break the flow but pollutes the console and Sentry-like listeners; likely from i18n / Intl.NumberFormat/DateTimeFormat with a bad locale string. Recommend guarding with try/catch and falling back to 'en-US'.

action_items: Investigate and fix the 'Incorrect locale information provided' pageerror thrown on the /auth/login and /auth/register pages (likely Intl usage with an empty/invalid locale from i18n).
critical_code_review_comments: GoogleSessionView sets cookie 'session_token' with samesite='none' and secure=True unconditionally — fine on the HTTPS preview, but will silently be dropped in local http:// dev; consider guarding with request.is_secure().
GoogleSessionView creates a new EmergentSession row on every call (no dedupe/cleanup); could accumulate rows on repeat logins. Consider upsert-by-session_token or a periodic cleanup task.

Frontend uses VITE_API_URL (not REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL) — that is fine for a Vite app, but the platform convention/testing rules reference REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL; documenting the mapping in README avoids confusion.
AuthCallbackPage stores JWTs in localStorage (XSS-vulnerable); acceptable given existing app design but worth noting.
test_credentials: talent.demo@joxera.test / DemoPass123! (talent), employer.demo@joxera.test / DemoPass123! (employer). Same as /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Registration test creates ephemeral users prefixed 'TEST_<uuid>@joxera.test'. No cleanup implemented — they are harmless and unique per run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend base URL: https://06f8fba5-b5a0-418f-97d9-f82b39c7023c.preview.emergentagent.com (also lives in frontend/.env VITE_API_URL). Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_auth_endpoints.py can be run with: `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_auth_endpoints.py -v`. Google OAuth cannot be end-to-end tested (real Google popup). We assert only the endpoint contract (400/401) and that the frontend button redirects to https://auth.emergentagent.com/?redirect=<origin>/auth/callback. The email-login → /dashboard/talent flow works.
